20260615---caterpillar---nrf54l15-dk-mot.filePath: 20260615 - Caterpillar - NRF54L15 DK motor drive test.md
20260615---caterpillar---nrf54l15-dk-mot.title: 20260615 - Caterpillar - NRF54L15 DK motor drive test
20260615---caterpillar---nrf54l15-dk-mot.content:
20260615 - Caterpillar - NRF54L15 DK motor drive verification
Setup
New NRF54L15 DK arrived and was tested with motor drive circuit:
PWM signal from DK (same code as Xinye's board)
  → STBB1-APUR breakout (voltage regulator)
  → DRV8212 breakout (motor driver)
  → LRA motor

Result
✅ Motor works perfectly — circuit feasibility verified.
Integrated Board Status

Xinye’s fully integrated board: IMU ✅, BLE ✅, potentiometer ✅ — only motor doesn’t work
Suspected cause: Some components not soldered well on the integrated board
Next step: Yibo has new clean PCBs — assemble more for comparison testing

Next Steps

Debug integrated board motor issue — compare against known-good DK breakout setup
Assemble new PCBs from Yibo for comparison/kaMoamoa/caterpillar/hardware/testing


Related

KaMoamoa MOC > Caterpillar

20260621---caterpillar---board-testing-a.filePath: 20260621 - Caterpillar - Board testing and MAX5419 saturation.md
20260621---caterpillar---board-testing-a.title: 20260621 - Caterpillar - Board testing and MAX5419 saturation
20260621---caterpillar---board-testing-a.content:
20260621 - Caterpillar - Board testing and MAX5419 saturation
Board Assembly Results


                  
                  Board 1 — assembly and testing 
                  
                

Most components worked after assembly. Motor does not work with 5V output from the boost converter (STBB1-APUR).




                  
                  Board 2 — assembly and testing 
                  
                

Initially didn’t work at all. After reflowing the MCU module with hot air gun → everything other than the MCU works. The 5V boost converter drop phenomenon (seen previously on board 1) recurred here: 5V drops to ~1.04V when the motor is connected.


STBB1-APUR Boost Converter Voltage Drop
Consistent across both boards: the boost converter’s 5V output collapses to ~1.04V under motor load.
MAX5419 / DRV8212 Voltage Saturation
There appears to be a threshold effect with the MAX5419-controlled voltage:


                  
                  MAX5419 voltage saturation data 
                  
                

Set-point < 3.1V  →  actual VDC consumed by DRV8212 is ~3.1V → motors move
Set-point > 3.1V  →  voltage drops to ~1.04V
Even with target voltage set to 2.5V, the VDC reads ~3.1V — needs double-check.


Highly likely that the MAX5419 digital potentiometer is causing the saturation behavior.
Previous Test Circuit Comparison


                  
                  Breakout-board test circuit — motor voltage comparison 
                  
                

Motor 1 VDC: 3.5V
Motor 2 VDC: 4.2V
Connected the earlier breakout-board-based test circuit with the same resistor set. The resistor set likely configures output to 4.2V, which would explain the discrepancy. Needs double-check.


Modular Redesign Plan
To isolate the root cause and enable independent subsystem testing, redesign the board into 5 small modular sub-boards connected by shunts:

MCU module (with LDO)
IMU module (switch to SPI instead of I2C)
Variable resistor module
DRV8212 module (motor driver)
STBB1-APUR module (boost converter)

Goal: tiny untethered boards with proper functional testing capability.
Key Suspect
The MAX5419 digital potentiometer is the prime suspect for the voltage saturation and drop issues.
Action Items

 Double-check the 2.5V set-point vs 3.1V actual VDC behavior
 Double-check whether the resistor set in the test circuit configures output to 4.2V
 Begin modular redesign into 5 sub-boards

Related

KaMoamoa MOC > Caterpillar
20260615 - Caterpillar - NRF54L15 DK motor drive test — Earlier motor drive verification

20260622---caterpillar---eval-board-stra.filePath: 20260622 - Caterpillar - Eval board strategy and MAX5417 resistance change.md
20260622---caterpillar---eval-board-stra.title: 20260622 - Caterpillar - Eval board strategy and MAX5417 resistance change
20260622---caterpillar---eval-board-stra.content:
20260622 - Caterpillar - Eval board strategy and MAX5417 resistance change
Strategy Change: Eval Boards Instead of Custom PCBs
Met with Yibo. Decided to purchase eval boards instead of ordering custom PCBs and soldering, enabling the modular sub-board testing plan from 20260621 - Caterpillar - Board testing and MAX5419 saturation.
Eval Boards Ordered






























Eval BoardPurposeNotesMAX5417LEVCMODUDigital potentiometer50K max resistance (vs 200K on MAX5419)STEVAL-ISA063V2Boost converterSTBB1-APUR evalSTEVAL-MKI193V1IMUSPI interface (was I2C before)NRF54L15 DKMCU moduleAlready have this
MAX5417 Resistance Change — Design Note
Since MAX5417 has 50K max resistance (vs 200K on MAX5419), the feedback resistor in the voltage divider needs to be replaced with a smaller value to keep the feedback loop correct.

20260624---caterpillar---ble-pwm-frequen.title: 20260624 - Caterpillar - BLE PWM frequency control via Python
20260624---caterpillar---ble-pwm-frequen.content:
20260624 - Caterpillar - BLE PWM frequency control via Python
Completed the BLE + PWM integration on the Caterpillar (NRF54L15). The motor PWM frequency can now be set over BLE via a Python script call that writes to the device — no recompile/reflash needed to change frequency. 🐛✅
This closes the loop on the modular eval-board test plan started in 20260622 - Caterpillar - Eval board strategy and MAX5417 resistance change: the DK can now be driven and tuned live from the host.

20260702---caterpillar---lipo-battery-te.filePath: 20260702 - Caterpillar - LiPo battery test fried inductor.md
20260702---caterpillar---lipo-battery-te.title: 20260702 - Caterpillar - LiPo battery test fried inductor
20260702---caterpillar---lipo-battery-te.content:
20260702 - Caterpillar - LiPo battery test fried inductor
Swapped the bench DC supply for a 150 mAh 30C LiPo to test the board on its real power source. (The DC supply was verified accurate against a multimeter — VDC matches set values ≤ 3.6 V.)
What happened

Motor didn’t vibrate strongly at first
Motor cycled on/off intermittently
Motor eventually stopped entirely
Board inspection: fried 0402 inductor MLZ1005M2R2WT000 (2.2 µH, 350 mA rating)

Root cause
30C × 150 mAh = 4.5 A peak battery capability, vs a 350 mA inductor rating — massively exceeded when the STBB1 draws high input current in boost mode at VDC ≥ 3.7 V with a high-C battery. (See 20260702 - Caterpillar - Why VDC saturates at 3.1V for why boost mode is where the input current spikes.)
Open investigation items

Peak current when setting VDC to a relatively high level.
Whether a 600 mA-rated 0402 inductor (highest available in 0402) would survive.
Capacitor voltage/current ratings under high-C battery operation.
Find a battery that won’t push peak current over 2.3 A (STBB1-APUR absolute max).
Overcurrent/overvoltage protection if no suitable battery exists in the form factor.

Related

KaMoamoa MOC > Caterpillar
20260702 - Caterpillar - Why VDC saturates at 3.1V — why boost-mode current spikes
20260702 - Caterpillar - Next board revision fixes — board-rev fixes this drives

20260702---caterpillar---next-board-revi.title: 20260702 - Caterpillar - Next board revision fixes
20260702---caterpillar---next-board-revi.content:
20260702 - Caterpillar - Next board revision fixes
Fix list for the next Caterpillar board spin, driven by the VDC-reset / 3.1 V-saturation fault.
Root cause (summary): the STBB1-APUR buck-boost enters its transition region when VDC ≈ battery voltage (~3.7 V for 1S LiPo under load). In boost mode input current exceeds output current and motor load grows as V² (~40 % more power at 3.7 V vs 3.1 V); the floating STBB1 MODE pin makes the transition region undefined; 0402 caps derate to ~6–8 µF at 3.7 V DC bias and can’t bridge ~4 ms PWM pulses at 113 Hz → +BATT sags → ADP2503-3.3 dips → nRF54L15 brown-out → POR (recovers at 3.1 V). Full analysis: 20260702 - Caterpillar - Why VDC saturates at 3.1V.
HW fixes (priority order)

Bulk capacitance on +BATT and VDC — ≥ 47–100 µF, larger package (0805/1206 MLCC, or tantalum/polymer to avoid DC-bias derating).
STBB1 MODE → tie to 3.3 V — forced PWM, stable behavior in all load conditions.
Shorter battery leads — reduce series inductance + resistance on the +BATT path.
100 k pulldown on STBB1 EN (P2.03) — during reset/bootloader the pin is Hi-Z and could float above the 1.2 V EN threshold.
Battery ADC divider — hi-Z divider from +BATT to a spare ADC pin (P1.09–P1.14) for a BLE battery service + low-battery cutoff.
15–20 kΩ series resistor between digipot H and STBB1 FB — hardware Vout ceiling as a backup to the firmware clamp.
Reverse-polarity protection on the 2-pin battery header.
Status LED — BLE state visibility without RTT.

IMU: high-rate readout (SPI + RISC-V coprocessor)

Wire the ASM330 IMU for SPI instead of I2C. The target sample rate is 600–800 Hz, which SPI handles comfortably where I2C would be marginal. (The SPI move was already flagged on the eval-board plan — 20260622 - Caterpillar - Eval board strategy and MAX5417 resistance change.)
Offload the reads to the nRF54L15 FLPR (RISC-V coprocessor): have the FLPR poll and buffer IMU samples at 600–800 Hz so the main application core is never interrupted by high-rate sampling — it consumes the buffered data on its own schedule.

Update — LiPo test findings (Jul 02)
Driven by the 20260702 - Caterpillar - LiPo battery test fried inductor failure:

Inductor: the 0402 MLZ1005M2R2WT000 (2.2 µH, 350 mA) fried under a 150 mAh 30C LiPo (4.5 A peak). The highest-rated 0402 is ~600 mA — still marginal → move to a larger package (0805/1206).
Capacitors: verify voltage/current ratings survive high-C battery operation — caps may also be under-rated.
Power-circuit redesign: fix the 3.7 V reset by redesigning the power circuit for the next rev rather than working around it in firmware.
Battery selection: choose a battery that won’t push peak current over 2.3 A (STBB1-APUR absolute max).
Protection circuit: if no suitable battery exists in the form factor, add overcurrent + overvoltage protection.

Related

KaMoamoa MOC > Caterpillar
20260702 - Caterpillar - Why VDC saturates at 3.1V — the diagnosis behind these fixes
20260622 - Caterpillar - Eval board strategy and MAX5417 resistance change

20260702---caterpillar---schematic-to-fi.title: 20260702 - Caterpillar - Schematic to firmware cross-check
20260702---caterpillar---schematic-to-fi.content:
20260702 - Caterpillar - Schematic to firmware cross-check
Full netlist cross-check of the Caterpillar KiCad schematic against the firmware (run with Claude Fable 5 via kicad-cli netlist export). Ignored the stale PCB.pdf from an old rev (MDBT42 module, MAX4624 switches, LTC3527).
Schematic components: Raytac AN54LQ-P15 (nRF54L15) module, STBB1-APUR (motor rail “VDC”), ADP2503-3.3 (system 3.3 V), DRV8212P H-bridge, ASM330LHH IMU, MAX5419 digipot, 10-pin Cortex SWD header.
Pin assignment — all confirmed matching


                  
                  Pin verification table 
                  
                










































NetModule pinFirmwareMatchI2C SDA / SCLP1.02 / P1.03 (NFC1/NFC2)i2c20 pinctrl✓IMU INT1 / INT2P1.04 / P1.05documented, unused (now fixed — INT1 wired for DRDY)✓DRV8212 nSLEEPP1.06driver_drv8212.c✓IN1 / IN2P1.07 / P1.08PWM ch0 / ch1✓STBB1 ENP2.03driver_stbb1_apur.c, active-high✓


Subtle things that lined up: SDA/SCL on NFC pins, devicetree correctly sets nfct-pins-as-gpios. IMU SA0→GND = address 0x6A, CS→3.3 V (I2C mode); digipot A0→GND = 0x28. IMU RES pins tied to 3.3 V (correct per ST datasheet — connect to VDDIO or GND). No 32 k crystal (XL1/XL2 unconnected) matches CONFIG_CLOCK_CONTROL_NRF_K32SRC_RC=y with lfxo disabled. L1 = 4.7 µH between DCC/DECD matches regulator-initial-mode = DCDC. Feedback network: R4 = 100 k from VDC to FB, digipot H→FB, W→GND, 200 k pot — formula in max5419.c confirmed: Vout = 0.5 × (1 + 100/R_HW), tap = 255 − 127.5/(2V−1).
Firmware fixes applied this session (9)

Digipot clamp — max5419_set_voltage() clamped at 4.2 V / tap ≤ 237. The digipot is the bottom leg of the STBB1 feedback divider: tap→255 shorts FB to GND and the boost runs toward its 5.5 V max into the motor. A single bad I2C write could do this — the clamp prevents it. (Previously had no upper clamp.)
Boot order — digipot programmed before DCDC enable, so the motor rail stays off if the digipot write fails; PWM boots in coast with explicit auto-start. Previously main() enabled STBB1 before programming the digipot, so VDC briefly sat at the MAX5419 power-on default (midscale ≈ 1.0 V) then jumped.
IMU DRDY interrupt — main loop paced by IMU DRDY on INT1/P1.04 (12.5 Hz, fresh samples) instead of blind 5 Hz polling. Fixes duplicate/stale samples from the 5 Hz-poll vs 12.5 Hz-ODR mismatch.
Gyro overflow — gyro fields widened to int32_t; mdps at ±250 dps overflowed int16_t beyond ~33 dps.
PWM per-channel + 4 Hz floor — drv_pwm_set_duty() now honors its channel argument (per-channel duty, enabling reverse drive later). Frequency floor raised to hardware-real 4 Hz, mirrored in the BLE handler and ble_control.py (which also validates one-shot --freq, since Write-Without-Response gives no error feedback).
BLE re-advertise — re-advertises after failed connection attempts, not just clean disconnects. Previously the device went dark after a failed pairing.
MAX5419 probe removal — removed a misplaced MAX5419 probe from the IMU driver (used an I2C read the chip doesn’t support — MAX5419 is write-only).
Scaffolding cleanup — removed the myfunc scaffold and the empty flpr/ placeholder.
CLAUDE.md correction — corrected the board reference (custom board, not the Raytac dev kit).

Plus: ble_control.py motor-voltage characteristic + --V flag added.
Related

KaMoamoa MOC > Caterpillar
20260702 - Caterpillar - Why VDC saturates at 3.1V — the power fault found this session
20260624 - Caterpillar - BLE PWM frequency control via Python

20260702---caterpillar---why-vdc-saturat.title: 20260702 - Caterpillar - Why VDC saturates at 3.1V
20260702---caterpillar---why-vdc-saturat.content:
20260702 - Caterpillar - Why VDC saturates at 3.1V
Earlier board testing (20260621 - Caterpillar - Board testing and MAX5419 saturation) turned up a puzzling behavior: VDC appeared to saturate at ~3.1 V — any set-point above that made the rail collapse to ~1.04 V — and the MAX5419 digipot was the prime suspect. This note explains the real mechanism (traced through the schematic with Claude Fable 5): it’s a brown-out reset, not digipot saturation.
The two observed numbers map cleanly onto the mechanism:

~3.1 V “saturation” = the value firmware reprograms on every boot (max5419_set_voltage(MOTOR_VDC_V) → tap 230). After any reset the rail lands back at 3.1 V, so it looks like a ceiling.
~1.04 V drop = the MAX5419 NV-EEPROM factory default (midscale, tap 128 ≈ 1.0 V), loaded when the digipot itself power-cycles on a deep brown-out.

A BLE write asking for VDC ≥ ~3.7 V is what trips the reset. 3.7 V isn’t a random number — it’s the 1S-LiPo terminal voltage under load, i.e. where the STBB1 boost converter’s input current runs away.
Power tree


                  
                  Shared-battery power tree 
                  
                

+BATT ──┬── U1 STBB1-APUR ──→ VDC ──→ DRV8212P ──→ motor
        └── U4 ADP2503-3.3 ──→ 3.3V ──→ nRF54L15, IMU, digipot


Both converters share one raw battery node via the M3 header. The MCU only resets if the 3.3 V rail dies, which only dies if +BATT itself collapses. That coupling through the shared battery node is the whole story.
Why exactly ~3.7 V

Below ~3.7 V VDC: STBB1 is in buck mode. Input current < output current (~85–95 %). Battery sees a modest, well-behaved load — 3.3 V works flawlessly.
At VDC ≈ V_batt: enters the buck-boost transition region; above it, boost mode. Two things happen at once:

Input current now exceeds output current: I_in ≈ I_out × V_out/(V_batt × η). Motor load also grows as V² — at 3.7 V it draws ~40 % more power than at 3.1 V.
The transition region (V_in ≈ V_out) is where every buck-boost has its worst efficiency and loop stability — and the STBB1 MODE pin is floating (confirmed in netlist), so power-save/PWM behavior in exactly this region is undefined.


+BATT sags. STBB1 input current spikes each PWM on-phase (50 % duty at ~113 Hz — long ~4 ms pulses caps can’t bridge). Support is only ~27 µF total on VDC in 0402 (derates to ~6–8 µF real at 3.7 V DC bias) + battery IR + thin headers.
ADP2503-3.3 follows. Also a buck-boost (needs V_in ≥ ~2.3 V, finite caps). When +BATT dips deep enough, 3.3 V drops below the nRF54L15 brown-out threshold → POR. Reboot reprograms the digipot to 3.1 V, STBB1 falls back to buck, everything recovers.

Why the firmware ramp fix didn’t help: 3.6 V vs 3.7 V is byte-identical bar one digipot tap. Ramping spreads the transition out, but boost-mode steady-state demand is what kills it — it dies roughly one tap after VDC crosses battery voltage.
Follow-up: MAX5419 reset behavior on brown-out (Fable)
Confirms where the 3.1 V recovery value actually comes from — it is not a digipot default.
The MAX5419 has no reset pin and doesn’t know the nRF54 rebooted. It only resets when its own supply (the 3.3 V rail) power-cycles below its POR threshold; on POR it loads the wiper from internal NV EEPROM, which still holds the factory default (midscale, tap 128 ≈ 1.0 V) because firmware never writes the NV register (CMD_V2NV is defined in max5419.c but never used).


                  
                  Two digipot outcomes on a brown-out 
                  
                


Shallow dip (3.3 V sags to ~1.7 V, MCU resets, digipot rides it out): wiper keeps its volatile value (e.g. tap 235 from mid-ramp). The digipot’s min operating voltage is 2.7 V, so in the 1.7–2.7 V gray zone the volatile state is undefined — but it does not go to 3.1 V by itself.
Deep dip (digipot PORs too): wiper reloads NV → tap 128 → rail ≈ 1.0 V. This is the ~1.04 V seen in testing.



Neither path produces 3.1 V. The 3.1 V comes from main.c: every boot, max5419_set_voltage(MOTOR_VDC_V) → tap 230 (RTT: “Target 3.10 V -> tap 230” within ~15 ms of the banner).
Sequence during a reset event: ramp reaches ~3.7 V → +BATT collapses → MCU brown-out → (digipot holds ~tap 235, or PORs to 128 → ~1.04 V) → firmware boots and overwrites the wiper to tap 230 → VDC = 3.1 V.
Implications:

The boot-order fix (digipot before STBB1 EN) is critical — a stale/default tap never reaches an enabled rail.
During the reset itself P2.03 is Hi-Z and STBB1 EN floats (no board pulldown); with unlucky leakage the converter could enable while the digipot still holds the pre-crash tap ~235 → reinforces the 100 k EN pulldown fix.
Programming the digipot NV EEPROM to 3.1 V (one-shot CMD_V2NV, ~50 k write endurance, via a BLE command or build flag) would make the power-up default 3.1 V instead of 1.0 V — defense in depth for that window.

Falsifiable predictions (not yet tested)

Fully charge the battery to 4.2 V → the reset point should shift up above ~4 V; 3.5 V and even 3.7 V requests should then start failing.
Scope +BATT and the 3.3 V rail while stepping 3.5 → 3.8 V → synchronized dips at the PWM rate on +BATT, then 3.3 V follows.
Bodge STBB1 MODE to 3.3 V (forced PWM) → should visibly raise the threshold (fixed-frequency, max dynamic performance per the ST datasheet), though it can’t fix the arithmetic of boost mode.

Related

KaMoamoa MOC > Caterpillar
20260621 - Caterpillar - Board testing and MAX5419 saturation — where the 3.1 V / 1.04 V behavior was first observed
20260702 - Caterpillar - Next board revision fixes — the HW/FW fixes this diagnosis drives
20260702 - Caterpillar - Schematic to firmware cross-check

20260704---caterpillar---board-redesign-.title: 20260704 - Caterpillar - Board redesign in KiCad
20260704---caterpillar---board-redesign-.content:
20260704 - Caterpillar - Board redesign in KiCad
Started the Caterpillar board redesign in KiCad (after testing the arrived boards). Planned changes:

Board shape — reshape so the SWD header becomes removable (detachable programming).
Component scaling — swap 0402 → 0603 passives for easier hand-assembly; switch to the 2.2 µH inductor used by the eval kit (the 0402 inductor fried under high current — see 20260702 - Caterpillar - LiPo battery test fried inductor).
Connectors — add castellated edges for the PWM outputs; replace the battery through-hole pads with a JST SH connector.
BOM review — pending.

These fold into the broader electrical fix list in 20260702 - Caterpillar - Next board revision fixes.
Related

KaMoamoa MOC > Caterpillar

20260706---caterpillar---motor-rail-fail.filePath: 20260706 - Caterpillar - Motor-rail failure root cause closed.md
20260706---caterpillar---motor-rail-fail.title: 20260706 - Caterpillar - Motor-rail failure root cause closed
20260706---caterpillar---motor-rail-fail.content:
20260706 - Caterpillar - Motor-rail failure root cause closed
Two new bench tests plus the Jul 4 LiPo test complete the picture on the VDC ≥ 3.7 V motor-rail failure. The nRF54 brown-out threshold is ~1.7 V (not at 3.3 V). Three sources, two failure modes, one root. Closes the investigation started in 20260702 - Caterpillar - Why VDC saturates at 3.1V.


                  
                  Three test sources → two failure modes 
                  
                






























SourceWhat happenedMechanismSurvivors600 mA limitSupply folds to 1.6 V → ADP2503 UVLO → MCU resetCurrent-starved, not over-currentL5 survives2 A limit~1 A draw, motor on/off cycling → L5 fails (65 kΩ)Saturation → thermal deathNo reset30C LiPoSame cycling, same L5 failure openSame thermal deathMatches 2 A test



Key mechanism
The 3.3 V rail dies via depth AND speed, not mode. +BATT must either plunge below the ADP2503’s ~2.3 V minimum input (UVLO — the converter gives up entirely), or dip so fast through lead inductance that the finite loop bandwidth + small output caps let the rail undershoot deeply before catching up. Raising the supply’s average current limit (600 mA → 2 A) doesn’t fix it because the killer is a microseconds-scale transient, not steady-state draw. The 1.6 V at reset = UVLO trace, confirmed.
Conclusions

MLZ1005M2R2WT000 (2.2 µH, 350 mA) is undersized — it saturates progressively from well below its rating; STBB1’s ~2 A switch limit then lets ~5–6× rated current flow, cremating it (measured 65 kΩ open).
Replacement must be a real power inductor: 2.2 µH, I_sat ≥ 2.5 A (above STBB1’s current limit, so the IC’s protection protects the inductor, rather than the inductor being the fuse). Candidates: TDK TFM201610ALMA2R2G, Murata DFE201612E-2R2M, Coilcraft XFL3012-222 — none fit 0402, plan pad rework.
MODE pin floating = undefined behavior in the buck-boost transition region; the eval board straps it → bodge to 3.3 V (forced PWM) for motor load.
Caps need bias-voltage headroom (a 10 µF MLCC at 4 V bias ≈ 3 µF effective).
EN pulldown (100 k to GND) keeps the motor rail off during MCU reset.
STBB1 is probably fine after two cremations — inspect for discoloration before next power-on.
The ASM330LHH NAK is an unrelated solder/attach issue on the IMU itself.

Minimal bench recipe to close the case

Desolder the L5 corpse (65 kΩ — motor rail is dead until it’s out).
Bodge a power inductor (2.2 µH, ≥ 2.5 A I_sat).
Bodge MODE to 3.3 V (forced PWM).
Verify caps have bias headroom, or tack on larger.
Feed from LiPo or a 2 A+ supply.
First test: 3.7 V with RTT attached — boot banner + sustained motor = case closed.

Related

KaMoamoa MOC > Caterpillar
20260702 - Caterpillar - Why VDC saturates at 3.1V — the investigation this closes
20260702 - Caterpillar - LiPo battery test fried inductor — the first inductor failure
20260706 - Caterpillar - Rev-2 electrical design complete — the fixes implemented in hardware

20260706---caterpillar---rev-2-electrica.title: 20260706 - Caterpillar - Rev-2 electrical design complete
20260706---caterpillar---rev-2-electrica.content:
20260706 - Caterpillar - Rev-2 electrical design complete
Full rev-2 electrical design completed in one session (7 commits): IMU migrated to dedicated SPI, motor-rail safety hardware added, independent design review passed, 4-layer layout with draft routing. Implements the fix list from 20260702 - Caterpillar - Next board revision fixes and the closure in 20260706 - Caterpillar - Motor-rail failure root cause closed.


                  
                  Commits (7 today) 
                  
                














































TimeHashContent11:48ed43b68Component placement/orientation (layout prep)15:5745b569cIMU I²C→SPI migration (CS=P1.09, SCLK=P1.10, MOSI=P1.13, MISO=P1.14); I²C retained for digipot only; C6 repurposed as IMU VDD_IO cap16:20edac98cVDC telemetry divider (100k/100k + 100nF → P1.11); STBB1 MODE/SYNC grounded16:58cf25ea5Motor-rail safety: R7 100k EN pulldown; R9 12.1k digipot-to-FB series resistor (HW VDC ceiling ~4.63V); C7 15pF feedforward removed17:50a491daaBoard synced to rev-2 (UUID relinks, orphan removed, 5 parts placed); BOM with sourced MPNs20:12fa59c394-layer conversion (In1=split power VDC/+3.3V/+BATT, In2=GND, 1.6mm stackup)22:1557b7f1cAll nets connected — draft routing complete


Key design decisions

Power isolation: parallel buck-boost architecture, per-converter input caps, stiff LiPo — the 3.3 V and 0–4.5 V rails won’t interfere; ground layout is the remaining variable.
Bug caught: MOSI double-connected / MISO floating in the first SPI draft.
Legacy revision list (8 items): EN pulldown ✔, MODE grounded ✔, reverse polarity via keyed JST ✔, digipot ceiling via R9 ✔; bulk caps + short leads obsoleted by the 30C pack; battery divider + status LED deliberately dropped (conditions recorded).
C7 removed: eval-board precedent + R9’s HF flooring of the FB leg; final verdict deferred to first-article scope test.
MLCC DC-bias: C9 (VDC 22 µF) upgraded to 10 V-rated 0603 to keep ≥ 10 µF effective at 4.63 V.
4-layer decision: the +4 SPI nets on a congested board tipped it (I²C stays for the digipot).

VDC sensing circuit
VDC telemetry divider added — review confirms the design is correct for its role (quasi-static rail monitoring):


                  
                  VDC_SENSE divider 
                  
                

VDC ──[R6 100k]──●── VDC_SENSE ──► P1.11 (ADC)
                 │
          [R5 100k]  [C13 100nF]
                 │        │
                GND      GND



Ratio: ÷2 → 4.5 V max VDC → 2.25 V at pin, safe inside SAADC range at 3.3 V supply ✓
Drain: 200 kΩ = 22.5 µA worst case, negligible vs 400 mA motor ✓
Filter: RC ≈ 5 ms (~32 Hz cutoff), good anti-alias; firmware needs ~40 µs SAADC acquisition time (50 kΩ Thevenin impedance).
Scope: measures quasi-static VDC after the digipot settles (wait ~5τ ≈ 25 ms after a setpoint change). Cannot see transients > 32 Hz (motor-kick ripple, PFM bursts, settling edges) — deliberate tradeoff, correct for telemetry.
Pin: P1.11 must be confirmed AIN-capable on nRF54L15 (AIN region ~P1.04–P1.14; verify against datasheet/devicetree before committing).
Escalation ladder if more capability is needed later: (1) 10k/10k divider + 1 nF → ~16 kHz BW; (2) add an op-amp buffer; (3) INA219/226 shunt on the motor path for real power monitoring.

Verification (tonight)

DRC: 0 unconnected, 0 schematic-parity issues ✓
Found: 1 /SDA via touching module XL1 pad, 1 stub, stale zone fills (cleared), ~66 mm SPI bus routed inside the In2 GND plane (flag #1 for the real layout pass).
Board outline self-intersecting — fix before fab.

Next session open items

Fix the /SDA-via short + DCC stub; repair the board outline.
Real layout: move SPI/I²C/sense routing out of the plane layers; consider GND on In1 (adjacent to top signals).
Sweep geometric DRC (edge clearance, courtyards, silk, mask expansion).
Fix 19 malformed vendor courtyards (scriptable).
Firmware: devicetree SPI + SAADC + digipot constants incl. R9; document “program digipot before raising EN.”
First-article bench: scope VDC sweep + motor start (C7 verdict), calibrate VDC_SENSE.

Related

KaMoamoa MOC > Caterpillar
20260704 - Caterpillar - Board redesign in KiCad — the mechanical/layout redesign started
20260702 - Caterpillar - Next board revision fixes — the fix list this implements
20260706 - Caterpillar - Motor-rail failure root cause closed — the failure these fixes address

20260707---caterpillar---rev-2-copper-la.filePath: 20260707 - Caterpillar - Rev-2 copper layout and DFM review.md
20260707---caterpillar---rev-2-copper-la.title: 20260707 - Caterpillar - Rev-2 copper layout and DFM review
20260707---caterpillar---rev-2-copper-la.content:
20260707 - Caterpillar - Rev-2 copper layout and DFM review
Rev-2 copper layout completed and hardened, then taken through a full DFM/mechanical review — fab-ready pending gerber export. Continues 20260706 - Caterpillar - Rev-2 electrical design complete (schematic/electrical) into the physical layout.
All nets routed, zones filled (commit 4d3edb5). A full subagent layout review (current paths, hot loops, plane integrity, decoupling proximity, thermal, mechanical) verified: converter cap loops tight, motor ground return clear of the IMU region, 23 well-distributed stitching vias.
Review criticals fixed

OUT1/OUT2 → 11 mil (~0.95 A @ 10 °C rise — passes 400 mA cont / 1 A stall)
STBB1 SW path → 13 mil (accepted: ~30 °C rise at worst-case 1.7 A boost stall, bounded by converter current limit + brief real stall durations)
U3 VM feed: 1 → 4 vias; +BATT into U1: 2 → 3 vias (stall current no longer via-bottlenecked)
VDC_SENSE rerouted 0.28 mm → ≥ 1.3 mm from the SW switching corridor
ADP2503 → L6 inductor tracks widened +1 mil
Commit 92288cb: thickened motor-driver power tracks, added VDC vias, moved VDC_SENSE off the SW2 corridor.

In2-plane SPI routing — conscious accept, then minimized
Slots in a plane force return-current detours → loops → EMI/pickup/crosstalk. Risk here is bounded by slow SPI edges, short runs, and stripline shielding of the buried tracks.
The In1 alternative was rejected: In1 sits only 0.1 mm under dense top-side signals (max return-path damage); its power zones carry stall current (slots = hidden constrictions); tracks inside the VDC zone would couple to the PWM-bouncing rail. The current placement on the far, current-free GND plane was already the least-bad home.
SPI runs shortened ~40 % (commit f638926): total inner-layer 65.4 → 45.2 mm (CS 15.9→9.4, SCLK 14.3→8.2, MOSI 12.9→8.6, MISO 12.5→8.9 mm).
SWD tab & antenna/RF

SWD tab & J2 edge overhang — intentional tear-off/snap-off design for the debug connector. The outline “mismatch” and connector overhang are by design and will be revised when the tab goes away. Removed from the concern list.
Antenna: plane copper under the module body is correct and stays (keepout applies only to the antenna section overhanging the board edge — proven on previous boards). A new 4×4 mm VLCF inductor sits 2.2 mm from the antenna edge on the back side: switching noise is out-of-band (non-issue); metal-mass detuning is plausible at a few dB → deferred to an RSSI A/B test at first article (cheap pre-qualification before any mid-layout relocation).

Evening DFM / mechanical review

Snap-off slot unroutable + planes crossing the snap line flagged; castellation holes off-edge (accepted, space).
Vias-in-pad, mask expansion killing solder dams under leadless parts.
Via surgery: 4 hole-spacing violations fixed; LGA-pad via pulled; 2 vias deleted. Rounds 1–2 created new shorts (caught by DRC-per-edit, reverted — lesson: dense boards need verification loops, not coordinate reasoning). LGA via deleted outright; VDD_IO fed by pour (verified).
Edge clearance: 14 errors → 0 via global 0.254 → 0.2 mm (= PCBWay routed minimum) + a scoped rule for by-design edge copper. Castellations need a min −1 mm rule (negative clearance).
Status LED (1da904f, 8bd8afc): P0.01 → 470 Ω → blue LED → GND, KiCad libs, scripted in. Netlist check caught a reversed-polarity first draft pre-commit. Placed/routed; polarity + routing verified.
DRC housekeeping: courtyard checks (malformed + overlap) demoted to warnings as a deliberate density policy; severity settings committed to the repo.
Pre-fab review: GO-WITH-CONDITIONS. Per-pin datasheet check of every IC passed; parity/fills/geometry clean; accepted trade-offs re-audited, none worse. Remaining: silk signature text too small to fab (fix before export); LED dim-but-OK. BOM edit pending.

Board renders (rev-2)
Top: 
Bottom:

index.title: Caterpillar — Dev Notes
index.content:
Caterpillar (working name) is a small, BLE-controlled vibrotactile board: an
nRF54L15 module driving a vibration motor through a DRV8212 H-bridge and an
STBB1-APUR boost rail, with an ASM330 IMU and a MAX5419 digital
potentiometer that sets the motor voltage. As of Jul 2026 the design is at
rev-2 — fab-ready pending gerber export.
Firmware lives in Moamoa_caterpillar_firmware.

Progress
graph LR
    A["First boards<br/>Jun"] --> B["Saturation<br/>mystery"] --> C["Root cause<br/>Jul 2"] --> D["Rev-2 design<br/>Jul 4–6"] --> E["Copper + DFM<br/>Jul 7"] --> F["Fab +<br/>first article"]
    classDef done fill:#2d6a4f,stroke:#1b4332,color:#ffffff;
    classDef current fill:#e76f51,stroke:#7d2408,color:#ffffff,stroke-width:4px;
    classDef next fill:transparent,stroke:#9aa0a6,stroke-width:1.5px,stroke-dasharray:5 4,color:#9aa0a6;
    class A,B,C,D done;
    class E current;
    class F next;

Status: rev-2 copper layout complete and DFM-reviewed → fab-ready pending gerber export. Next: fabrication + first-article bring-up.
This is a running development log — newest first.
Jul 2026 — Rev-2 redesign

20260707 - Caterpillar - Rev-2 copper layout and DFM review — copper layout hardened and taken through a full DFM/mechanical pass (route criticals, plane-slot SPI tradeoff, via surgery, edge clearance, status LED). Fab-ready pending gerber. Includes board renders.
20260706 - Caterpillar - Rev-2 electrical design complete — the rev-2 schematic in one session: IMU moved to dedicated SPI, motor-rail safety hardware, VDC-sense telemetry, 4-layer stackup.
20260706 - Caterpillar - Motor-rail failure root cause closed — the failure is fully explained: an undersized power inductor (L5) + floating MODE pin + insufficient cap bias headroom; a microseconds-scale UVLO transient, not steady-state draw.
20260704 - Caterpillar - Board redesign in KiCad — mechanical redesign kickoff: removable SWD tab, 0402→0603 passives, castellated PWM edges, JST-SH battery connector.

Jul 2026 — Diagnosis: the power fault

20260702 - Caterpillar - Why VDC saturates at 3.1V — the headline puzzle: the motor rail seemed stuck at ~3.1 V (and collapsed to ~1 V above it). It’s a brown-out reset, not the digipot — traced through the schematic.
20260702 - Caterpillar - LiPo battery test fried inductor — a 30C LiPo fried the 0402 inductor; peak-current analysis vs the 350 mA part.
20260702 - Caterpillar - Schematic to firmware cross-check — full netlist ↔ firmware pin verification, plus a batch of firmware fixes.
20260702 - Caterpillar - Next board revision fixes — the prioritized hardware fix list that rev-2 implements.

Jun 2026 — First boards & the saturation mystery

20260624 - Caterpillar - BLE PWM frequency control via Python — set the motor PWM frequency live over BLE.
20260622 - Caterpillar - Eval board strategy and MAX5417 resistance change — switch to eval boards for modular subsystem testing.
20260621 - Caterpillar - Board testing and MAX5419 saturation — first assembled boards: VDC appears to saturate at ~3.1 V (the mystery solved above).
20260615 - Caterpillar - NRF54L15 DK motor drive test — initial motor-drive bring-up on the dev kit.
